text: You see only me because your heart knows only me as the Companions leader.
I'd wager old Vignar could see half a dozen of my predecessors.
And I see them all. The ones in Sovngarde. The ones trapped with me in Hircine's realm.
And they all see you. You've brought honor to the name of the Companions. We won't soon forget it.
And that's exactly what the old man wanted. I'm glad we could give it to him.
He's not a werewolf any more? Even dead? This is a good thing!
Yes, you brought honor to him, even after his death. A worthy outcome for a worthy warrior.
I'll be honest, I wouldn't have expected it.
But that may just be because I remember when you were just a whelp who was begging to join.
The old man trusted you, so I'll trust you.
Oh, you don't have to worry about any problems from me. I do what I'm told. Congratulations!
If you had told me a few months ago that some outsider I had never heard of would lead the Companions, I might have slit your throat.
But I've seen what you can do. And heard of the love that Kodlak had for you. The trust.
Let's hope you can show that his heart was not deceived. Good luck, my friend.
Eventually. But this... this is the Tomb of Ysgramor. I think I'm just going to... commune for a bit.
This place is worthy of some time. You go on ahead. I'll see you back there.
I go where my brother goes. I think he wants to look around some more.
Not right now. I'm going to look at some of these carvings. I wonder if Ysgramor ever set foot here while he was alive.
But you don't have to stay, if you don't want to. I'll see you back at Jorrvaskr. Stories to tell the others, eh?
Ah, I can't reveal all my secrets to you. But rest assured, I would know.
But how would you know it's real?
Elda, you know better than to believe every idiot traveler that wanders through.
Captain, have you heard these stories of dragons?
But they're saying that one of them freed Ulfric... that he's in league with them.
Think about how ridiculous that sounds. Come now.
Calixto, do you know if there's any truth to these rumors of dragons?
Eh. I try not to trouble myself with stories of the outside world.
Now if someone brings me a dragon scale, that's a different story.
I'm fairly sure that the dragons don't care about our matters one way or the other.
But just think of the advantage.
Do you think we could persuade one of the dragons to join our side?
I'm not saying it wouldn't be useful. But there are better uses of our resources right now.
I don't suppose we would ever really trust the thing anyway.
I am Lu'ah Al-Skaven. Who are you to disrupt my work? Begone, or I will add you to my army of the dead!
Tythis, please. The Snow-Shods are good people, but tend to exaggerate. Try and stay calm for your own sake.
We hold each other close and hope that all of this ends well.
Yes, milady. I've also taken the liberty in preparing a private carriage... just in case.
Yes, milady. That unpleasantness at Helgen supposedly involved a dragon. It killed several soldiers before it departed.
Talen, do you believe what they're saying? Do you really think the dragons have returned?
Madesi, have you heard the Imperials might be headed this way? What would we do?
Good. If Riften should fall, it's only right that its leader be spared.
I don't know what to believe. If it's true, then dark times may be upon us.
They aren't invading, milord... it's simply a response to Ulfric and his Stormcloaks. I assure you everything will be fine.
I want you to speak to the city guard. Make sure there's a contingency plan in effect in case Riften falls under attack.
But I've heard from the Snow-Shods that they intend to burn this place to the ground!
Have our scouts come up with any information on these reports of dragons?
What should we do?
See if you can unlock that door.
Did you find a key? See if it unlocks that door.
Unlock that door and let's get out of here.
You have your orders. Rendezvous with the rest of the men outside Korvanjund.
What did you need, soldier?
I knew I heard something. What's this bridge doing down?
So, stop your worrying and get some rest. Your shift is coming up and I don't want you dozing off again like last time.
Keep looking. Someone's here, I can feel it.
This again? I told you we have someone standing guard out there. And, don't forget the rock trap we rigged up.
Anything?
No, haven't seen anyone.
We did! Keep your eyes peeled. I don't think we're alone. You check the entrance. I'm going to look over here.
Aren't you worried someone will wander in here? The entrance isn't exactly hidden you know.
